reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: The pattern across three games is unmistakable. Games 1 and 2, I played cautiously — verifying answers, waiting for consensus — and got randomly eliminated in Round 2 both times, ending with 0 coins. Game 3, I volunteered Round 1 with all single-endorsed capsules and got it right, keeping 4 coins and saving everyone. These are simple arithmetic puzzles. My answer (85 - 47 = 38) is trivial, and the others' puzzles are similarly straightforward. Across all three prior games, roughly 11 out of 12 initial answers were correct. The expected value of volunteering now (~90% × 4 coins = 3.6) massively outweighs the cautious approach, which historically has given me 0 coins twice and risks random elimination every round we delay. The math is clear: volunteer early, save everyone, keep all coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: Round 1 with all four questions having only single unverified capsules — the same starting position as every previous game. My answer (50 - 23 = 27) is sealed in Q2-B. While Game 3 showed that early volunteering can pay off with simple arithmetic puzzles, I can't see the other puzzles without verifying, so I can't assess their difficulty or likelihood of error. My proven strategy of verifying Q1 first has worked consistently across three games. This costs 1 coin (4→3) but lets me independently solve Player A's puzzle and confirm or flag their answer. I expect other players will verify my Q2 and other questions in parallel, building consensus organically. If the pattern holds, someone may even volunteer this round with partial verification (as A did in Game 3). If not, I'll verify Q4 in Round 2 (the highest-risk capsule if D gets eliminated) and volunteer in Round 3 with strong confidence.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q4
REASONING: Following the successful pattern from Game 3 where early Q4 verification enabled A to volunteer in Round 1 and save everyone. In Games 1 and 2, waiting too long to verify Q4 led to D being eliminated and costly multi-round delays. D's Q4 answer was actually wrong in Game 1, so there's real precedent for errors there. By verifying Q4 now (4→3 coins), I address the historically weakest link early. If B follows their consistent pattern of verifying Q1, then A will see Q1-A+B (2 endorsements) and Q4-D+C (2 endorsements), plus their own Q1 and Q2-B — the same confidence level that led A to successfully volunteer in Round 1 last game. My own answer (71-28=43) I'm 100% confident about. This strategy maximizes the chance of a Round 1 escape while spending minimal coins, potentially saving all 4 players again.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
